title: Bridge Essay: Cohesion and Joint Speech: Right Hemisphere Contributions to Synchronized Vocal

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Language/Mantra** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Phase Language/Mantra bridge-v3 targeted acquisition.

**Cohesion and Joint Speech: Right Hemisphere Contributions to Synchronized Vocal Production.** — The Journal of neuroscience : the official journal of the Society for Neuroscience (2016). PMID: 27122026. DOI: 10.1523/JNEUROSCI.4075-15.2016. Score: 30. Synchronized behavior (chanting, singing, praying, dancing) is found in all human cultures and is central to religious, military, and political activities, which require people to act collaboratively and cohesively; however, we know little about the neural underpinnings of many kinds of synchronous behavior (e.g., vocal behavior) or its role in establishing and maintaining group cohesion.
